records.display_name: Employee Safety Call Center

payload.claim_text: The NYCT Employee Safety Call Center provides personnel with an additional process to report a non-emergency safety concern. The call center is not intended to replace departmental incident reporting protocols for unsafe conditions. Employees can also call back to the hotline to find out more information about their incident using a reference number.

records.display_name: NYCT and MTA Bus operational scope

payload.claim_text: New York City Transit Bus and MTA Bus operate 5,932 buses on 238 local, 20 Select Bus Services (SBS) and 75 express bus routes in all five boroughs. The average weekday ridership for 2024 was 1.3 million a day, 409 million annually. The number of average weekday trips is approximately 54,000 servicing 16,000 bus stops. Buses are housed, washed, serviced, and maintained at 27 depots.

records.display_name: ASP approved by NYCT Safety Committee

payload.claim_text: As designated in the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act) the NYCT Agency Safety Plan has been approved by NYCT Safety Committee (Safety Committee) and is memorialized in the approved which can be found on page 2-4.

records.display_name: MTA Board approval pending

payload.claim_text: Approval by the MTA Board of Directors: TO BE ADDED

records.display_name: Zero-Emission Fleet mandate by 2040

payload.claim_text: Ensures to achieve mandated Zero-Emission Fleet by year 2040.

records.display_name: Approval documentation pending

payload.claim_text: Updated approval documentation on pages 2-4 thru 2-5 – To be added once ASP approved.

records.display_name: Get It Fixed Reporting Website

payload.claim_text: Get It Fixed is an online reporting portal developed for use by NYCT employees to report issues like customer behavior, equipment issues, climate/comfort, and cleanliness among other reportable topics. Located at https://mta.info/getitfixed

records.display_name: Workplace Violence Prevention Program

payload.claim_text: DOB follows the most recent version of Workplace Violence Prevention Program Policy Instruction #1.20 (Appendix AP). NYCT created a multi-faceted Workplace Violence Prevention Program. NYCT maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward acts of violence.

records.display_name: De-escalation training for all bus operators

payload.claim_text: All bus operators are trained biennially on the challenges of customer interaction and presented with mitigation techniques aimed at minimizing employee assaults. All NYCT employees are trained on and required be familiar with the agency policy to promote a safe environment for all employees. Maintenance employees take an initial workplace violence prevention training P/I #P-1 training and complete an annual workplace violence prevention policy review.
